perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means all financial, business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party solely in connection with the Evaluation, except that such term will not include (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality, (ii) information that is or becomes publicly known other than through a breach by the Receiving Party of any of its obligations under this Agreement, (iii) information received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not known by the Receiving Party to be under an obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party, (iv) information independently developed by the Receiving Party without reliance upon the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and (v) information that the Disclosing Party has disclosed to other parties without similar obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means all financial, business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party. Confidential Information excludes (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clauses (ii), (iii), (iv) and (v) from the 'Confidential Information' definition, this perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. Now, the definition does not specify exceptions for information that becomes publicly known, is received from a third party without confidentiality obligations, independently developed, or disclosed to other parties without similar obligations. This contradicts the later statements that implicitly rely on these exceptions. This lack of clarity undermines the overall purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto acknowledge that they may compete in certain lines of business. Nothing herein shall be construed to prevent the parties from continuing to so compete or from continuing to carry out their respective plans and objectives, provided that the parties comply with the terms of this Agreement. Further, the terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either party's right to independently develop or acquire products, functionality or services of the same type as may be included within any Confidential Information or to enter into any business transaction with any other company which owns or has rights to any such similar products or services, as long as such right is exercised without the use of any other party's Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing several key exceptions present in the original text. Specifically, it eliminates exceptions for information that becomes publicly known, is received from a third party without confidentiality obligations, is independently developed, or is disclosed to other parties without similar obligations. This creates a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text,' which allows parties to 'independently develop or acquire products, functionality or services of the same type as may be included within any Confidential Information' as long as it's done 'without the use of any other party's Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.' By removing the exception for independently developed information, the changed definition now makes it impossible for a party to independently develop similar products without violating the agreement, as anything related to those products would now be considered confidential information, directly contradicting the allowance for independent development stated in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used for internal research purposes to improve or innovate upon either parties' own offerings.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration to Section 2 introduces a contradiction. Initially, the 'Confidential Information' was restricted solely to the evaluation of the Partner’s Application within the ADP Marketplace. The modified text now allows the use of this information for general internal research and development. This directly contradicts the original intention of restricted use, creating uncertainty about the permitted uses of 'Confidential Information'. The ambiguity is now that the Receiving party has two different ways on utilizing the 'Confidential Information' which one to abide by.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by expanding the permitted use of 'Confidential Information' beyond the initial scope of evaluating the Partner's Application in the ADP Marketplace. The contradicted text explicitly restricts the use of Confidential Information to 'evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above'. The changed text allows for internal research, creating a direct conflict with the original restricted use case as detailed in the original and contradicted sections.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using a level of care and discretion solely determined by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the standard of care required for protecting Confidential Information. The original text sets a 'reasonable care and discretion' floor, tied to how the Receiving Party protects its own information. The revised text allows the Receiving Party to unilaterally determine the level of care, potentially lowering it. This contradicts Section 7's expectation of reasonable cooperation and can undermine the Disclosing Party's confidence in the protection of their Confidential Information. This creates ambiguity in how the Confidential information is handled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'reasonable care and discretion,' setting a minimum standard for protecting confidential information. The changed text allows the Receiving Party to unilaterally determine the level of care, potentially lowering it below 'reasonable'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which requires the Receiving Party to 'reasonably cooperate' in the event of a breach. If the Receiving Party is allowed to define any level of care, even below reasonable care, their cooperation might not be considered 'reasonable', which create inconsistency and contradiction. Thus, the changed text alters the standard of care, undermining the 'reasonable cooperation' requirement, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction as the change breaks consistency and introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects a level of certainty and reasonableness.

perturbation.original_text: Further, this Agreement and any meetings or communications of the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement will not (i) constitute any offer, request, or contract among the parties to engage in any transaction, nor (ii) constitute any offer, request or contract involving a buyer-seller relationship, venture, teaming or partnership relationship among the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Further, this Agreement and any meetings or communications of the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement will (i) constitute an agreement to negotiate in good faith towards a potential future transaction, nor (ii) constitute a non-binding expression of interest in exploring a buyer-seller relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 5 introduces an in-text contradiction by suggesting the agreement does constitute an agreement to negotiate and an expression of interest in a buyer-seller relationship. The original text explicitly states that the agreement does *not* constitute any offer, request, or contract for any transaction or relationship. The altered text changes this. This creates uncertainty and potential legal disputes about the nature of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties as to the subject matter hereof and merges and supersedes all prior discussions, agreements and understandings of any kind and every nature, whether written or oral, between them.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement does *not* constitute any offer, request, or contract for any transaction or relationship. The changed text states that the agreement *will* constitute an agreement to negotiate and an expression of interest in a buyer-seller relationship. The contradicted text states that the agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties as to the subject matter hereof and merges and supersedes all prior discussions, agreements and understandings of any kind and every nature, whether written or oral, between them. The change introduces a direct conflict with the contradicted text as the agreement now reflects a possible negotiation/transaction when it originally did not.
